meta.tagline: The PNW startup pitch competition directory

events.name: Demolicious Monthly Pitch
events.description: Portland's monthly pitch event reintroduced in 2024 by UpStart Collective. Founders get 3 minutes to pitch, audience votes for the winner. Monthly champion advances to an annual Championship Showdown.
events.notes: Actively accepting pitch applications for 2026. Sign up at their website.

events.name: Westside Pitch
events.description: Pitch competition for Washington County, OR entrepreneurs. Offers equity investment, cash prizes, and professional services to tech and traded-sector startups.
events.notes: April 23, 2026 in Hillsboro. Applications closed March 9 — monitor for next cycle.

events.name: Columbia River Pitch
events.description: Pitch competition for entrepreneurs in the Columbia River region of Oregon and SW Washington.
events.notes: No 2026 date announced yet. Monitor TiE Oregon's events page.

events.name: Collegiate Startup Challenge
events.description: Statewide pitch competition for Oregon and SW Washington college and university entrepreneurs including students, faculty, and alumni.
events.notes: No 2026 date announced yet. Monitor TiE Oregon's events page.

events.name: Pitch Please
events.description: Monthly pitch event where founders get 5 minutes to pitch virtually or in person. Regional thought leaders ask questions and give feedback.
events.notes: Runs monthly. Check OEN calendar for next date.

events.name: Angel Oregon Technology
events.description: 5-week intensive program for early-stage technology founders culminating in a live Pitch Day in front of active investors. Investment announced in August 2026.
events.notes: 2026 program active now. Pitch Day April 7th.

events.name: Angel Oregon CPG
events.description: 5-week intensive program for early-stage consumer packaged goods founders culminating in a live Pitch Day in front of active investors. Investment announced in June 2026.
events.notes: 2026 program active now. Pitch Day April 8th.

events.name: Angel Oregon BIO
events.description: 5-week intensive program for early-stage life science and bioscience founders culminating in a live Pitch Day in front of active investors. Investment announced in December 2026.
events.notes: 2026 program active now. Pitch Day April 9th.

events.name: Pop Up & Pitch
events.description: Marketplace pitch events for Oregon food and beverage entrepreneurs, co-hosted with partners like Burgerville.
events.notes: Typically held spring/summer. Monitor OEN calendar.

events.name: PSU Pitch Events
events.description: Portland State University's Center for Entrepreneurship is a founding partner of Invent Oregon and hosts its own pitch and entrepreneurial programming throughout the academic year.

events.name: InventOR Collegiate Challenge
events.description: Oregon's only statewide collegiate prototyping competition. Student teams receive $2,000 development grants and compete for $30,000 in prizes at the annual June finals.
events.notes: 2026 finals scheduled for June. Bootcamp in May. Apply through your school.

events.name: OBI Pitch Events
events.description: Oregon's life sciences incubator occasionally hosts pitch and demo events for biotech and health-focused startups. Also a past venue for Founders Live PDX.

events.name: 45Camp Accelerator
events.description: VertueLab's accelerator program supporting early-stage cleantech startups with mentorship, curriculum, connections, and pitch-based funding opportunities.
events.notes: Accelerator program runs May–September. Watch for application opening in spring.

events.name: Cascadia CleanTech Accelerator
events.description: 18-week virtual accelerator for pre-seed cleantech startups in the Northwest. Includes mentorship, curriculum, and pitch-based funding competition. Program runs May–September annually.
events.notes: Applications typically open in spring for May start. Monitor their site.

events.name: Portland Startup Week 2026
events.description: Week-long celebration of Portland's startup community including pitch events, workshops, and networking. Includes the annual Demolicious Startup Week edition.
events.notes: May 11–16, 2026. Event submissions open — check their site.

events.name: Founders Live Build — Portland
events.description: New 2026 format: 5 founders build apps live from a prompt while attendees network, then demo and pitch. Audience votes for the winner.
events.notes: April 9, 2026 at Metro Region Innovation Hub, 2130 SW 5th Ave Portland. 6–9pm.

events.name: Founders Live Seattle
events.description: Monthly happy-hour pitch competition where 5 founders each give a 99-second pitch followed by audience Q&A. Audience votes determine the winner. Seattle is the birthplace of Founders Live.
events.notes: Last event February 26, 2026. No next date announced — monitor their events page.

events.name: Gorge PitchFest 2026
events.description: Annual pitch competition hosted by the Mid-Columbia Innovation Hub serving entrepreneurs in the Columbia River Gorge region on both the Oregon and Washington sides.
events.notes: May 28, 2026 at The Granada Theatre, The Dalles. Applications open now.

events.name: Beaverton Startup Challenge
events.description: Annual funding competition operated by the Oregon Startup Center in partnership with the City of Beaverton and the Westside Startup Fund. Open to scalable startups across tech, consumer products, manufacturing, bio/life sciences, and cleantech. Winners receive a SAFE note investment (historically ~$20k), 6 months of mentorship, and a Demo Day showcase.
events.notes: Applications closed March 27, 2026. Event/Demo Day upcoming — check their site for date.

events.name: Impact Pitch Competition
events.description: Annual multi-round pitch competition open to small businesses in Alaska, Idaho, Oregon, and Washington. Focuses on community impact alongside business viability. Three categories: Startup/Idea Stage, Early Stage, and Established. Note: tech companies and apps are not eligible.
events.notes: 2025 competition concluded October 9. Watch for 2026 applications to open around May–June 2026.

events.name: Venture Community
events.description: Oregon’s original reverse road show event bringing together 12 leading PNW funds and 26 Oregon startups for 2 days.
events.notes: Last event was in 2018.

events.name: PIE Demo Day
events.description: One of Portland's most established startup incubators, co-founded by Wieden+Kennedy. Has hosted demo days and pitch events for cohorts since 2009.
events.notes: All PIE programs listed as 'on hiatus for 2026' on their website. Worth monitoring for a return.

events.name: Dempsey Startup Competition
events.description: One of the Pacific Northwest's premier collegiate startup competitions, open to students at accredited colleges across the Cascadia Corridor (WA, OR, ID, BC, AK). Teams develop business plans and pitch to investors across multiple elimination rounds.
events.notes: Applications due April 6, 2026. Competition rounds April 1 – May 21, 2026. Open to Oregon students.

events.name: SCORE Washington Women's Pitch Contest
events.description: Annual pitch competition for women entrepreneurs in Washington, Alaska, and Northern Idaho. Focused on helping women-led businesses secure loan financing. Finalists pitch directly to bank and financial institution loan officers.
events.notes: 2026 final event on 4.2.

events.name: SCORE Tech Entrepreneurs Pitch Contest
events.description: Annual pitch competition for Washington-based tech entrepreneurs at any stage. Participants work with SCORE mentors to develop pitch decks, then compete in front of judges for cash prizes and mentorship.
events.notes: Pitch deck submissions due July 31, 2026. Free to enter. Work with a SCORE mentor to prepare.

events.name: TAO AI Startup Innovation Sandbox
events.description: Live pitch competition showcasing early-stage AI startups with Pacific Northwest roots. Features an investor reverse pitch panel, a live startup pitch competition with investor voting, and networking with founders, investors, and regional innovation leaders.
events.notes: April 22, 2026 in Portland. Open to pre-seed and seed-stage AI startups with PNW roots.

aggregators.name: Calagator
aggregators.description: Portland's community tech calendar. A great first stop for spotting new pitch and startup events.

aggregators.name: Silicon Forest
aggregators.description: Portland's best local startup news source — excellent coverage of pitch events, demo days, and ecosystem news.

aggregators.name: Technology Association of Oregon (TAO)
aggregators.description: Oregon's leading tech industry association. Check their calendar for pitch and innovation events.
